text:
Once there was an old bookstore owner named Mrs. Whatsit. She lived up high in an old mansion with her friend Mrs Whatsit.

Mrs Whatsit liked feathers. You wouldn't believe how many wild feathers she collected!.

Every afternoon she'd fly downstairs and fill colorful beanbags she designed herself full.

Then Mrs Whatsit would sit counting feathers downstairs until midnight. She liked that best.

Every night she'd take a nap beneath an apple tree. Then Mrs Whatsit would go out and hunt.

One evening when Mrs Whatsit flew out she saw a duckling lonely as dusk.

"A bird?" Mrs Whatsit said.

"Yeah!" duckling cried.

"What'd you steal?"

"Duck eggs!" shrieked ducklings together.

Mrs Whatsit thought.

"We could hatch some! Ducklings!"

"Yeah!" Everyone nodded.

"You gather enough little yolks."

They flew nighty-night.

That summer Mrs Whatsit baked cakes.

The yolks were delicious.

One night Mrs Whatsit just knew one
